Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 255 upvotes, 18 comments
  2. Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse 544 upvotes, 79 comments
  3. Optimizing Top K in Postgres 128 upvotes, 15 comments
  4. Caxlsx: Ruby gem for xlsx generation with charts, images, schema validation 37 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 109 upvotes, 25 comments
  6. LoGeR – 3D reconstruction from extremely long videos (DeepMind, UC Berkeley) 105 upvotes, 24 comments
  7. Sending Jabber/XMPP Messages via HTTP 24 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. JSLinux Now Supports x86_64 360 upvotes, 116 comments
  9. Two Years of Emacs Solo 319 upvotes, 118 comments
  10. Amazon is holding a mandatory meeting about AI breaking its systems 71 upvotes, 21 comments
  11. Lotus 1-2-3 on the PC with DOS 154 upvotes, 58 comments
  12. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" (2009) 190 upvotes, 75 comments
  13. Practical Guide to Bare Metal C++ 78 upvotes, 28 comments
  14. I put my whole life into a single database 285 upvotes, 131 comments
  15. PgAdmin 4 9.13 with AI Assistant Panel 41 upvotes, 14 comments
  16. Yann LeCun's AI startup raises $1B in Europe's largest ever seed round 307 upvotes, 163 comments
  17. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 203 upvotes, 104 comments
  18. Show HN: DD Photos – open-source photo album site generator (Go and SvelteKit) 24 upvotes, 7 comments
  19. TCXO Failure Analysis 73 upvotes, 33 comments
  20. No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user 373 upvotes, 265 comments
  21. Rebasing in Magit 91 upvotes, 56 comments
  22. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 36 upvotes, 18 comments
  23. How many options fit into a boolean? 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Meta acquires Moltbook 41 upvotes, 24 comments
  25. A New Version of Our Oracle Solaris Environment for Developers 25 upvotes, 13 comments
  26. RFC 454545 – Human Em Dash Standard 48 upvotes, 33 comments
  27. Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft 539 upvotes, 538 comments
  28. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 279 upvotes, 284 comments
  29. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 55 upvotes, 48 comments
  30. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 11 upvotes, 14 comments

Last generated 12:00am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
